mnemonics: TIDY Tabs: Think of structured data like a tidy spreadsheet with rows (Tabs) and columns.
keyPoints: Structured data is organized in a tabular format with rows and columns.
Each row typically represents an observation or record.
Each column represents a specific variable or feature with a defined data type.
commonMistakes: Treating structured data as unstructured, like trying to analyze a spreadsheet as a block of text.
Ignoring data types, such as processing numerical values as strings or vice versa.
Forgetting to check for and handle missing values within the structured table.
